subject: Physics
solution:
The wire are joined end-to-end, so they are in series combination, then total length of wire is given by
L=L1+L2  ...(1)

When the temperature is increase by ∆θ, then its final length is given by using concept of linear expansion,
L'=L(1+α∆θ)  ...(2)

Using equation (1) and (2) for the series combination of wires, final length is given by

L'=L1'+L2'  ...(3)

⇒L1+L2(1+αeff∆θ)=L1(1+α1∆θ)+L2(1+α2∆θ)

⇒αeff=L1α1+L2α2L1+L2

subject: Physics
solution:
According to Newton's law of cooling,
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\left(\frac{\theta_1-\theta_2}{t}\right)=K\left(\frac{\theta_1+\theta_2}{2}-\theta_0\right) \\
&\left(\frac{60-50}{10}\right)=K\left(\frac{60+50}{2}-25\right) \\
&\text { and, }\left(\frac{50-\theta}{10}\right)=K\left(\frac{50+\theta}{2}-25\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
Dividing eq. (i) by (ii),
$$
\frac{10}{(50-\theta)}=\frac{60}{\theta} \Rightarrow \theta=42.85^{\circ} \mathrm{C} \cong 43^{\circ} \mathrm{C}
$$

subject: Physics
solution:
Given $R_0=8 \Omega, R_{100}=10 \Omega$ $\therefore \mathrm{R}_{100}=\mathrm{R}_0(1+\alpha \Delta \mathrm{T})$
Also, $R_{400}=R_0\left(1+\alpha \Delta \mathrm{T}^1\right)$ $\begin{aligned} & \therefore 10=8(1+\alpha \times 100) \Rightarrow 100 \alpha=\frac{1}{4} \\ & \therefore \mathrm{R}_{400}=8(1+400 \alpha)=8(1+1)=16 \Omega \end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
solution:
Given:

∆Q=184×103 J,

m=0.600 kg at -12 °C,

S=2222.3 J kg–1 °C–1 and

L=336×103J kg-1

Heat required to convert ice from –12 °C to ice at 0 °C will be, 

Q1=0.600×2222.3×12=16000.56 J

Remaining heat Q=184000-16000.56

= 167999.44 J

Heat required to melt ice completely at 0 °C,

Q2 = 0.600 × 336000 = 201600 J needed which is more than Q, so 100% ice is not melted.

For amount of melted ice, we can write

167999.44=m×336000⇒m= 0.4999 kg

∴ mass of water =0.4999 kg

Mass of ice remaining = 0.1001

∴ Ratio =0.10010.4999≈1:5

subject: Physics
solution:
As $1 \mathrm{~g}$ of steam at $100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ melts $8 \mathrm{~g}$ of ice at $0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.
$10 \mathrm{~g}$ of steam will melt $8 \times 10 \mathrm{~g}$ of ice at $0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ Water in calorimeter $=500+80+10 \mathrm{~g}=590 \mathrm{~g}$

subject: Physics
solution:
From Newton's law of cooling,
$$
t=\frac{1}{k} \log _e\left(\frac{\theta_2-\theta_0}{\theta_1-\theta_0}\right)
$$
From question and above equation,
$$
5=\frac{1}{k} \log _e \frac{(40-30)}{(80-30)}
$$
And, $t=\frac{1}{k} \log _e \frac{(32-30)}{(62-30)}$
Dividing equation (2) by (1),
$$
\frac{t}{5}=\frac{\frac{1}{k} \log _e \frac{(32-30)}{(62-30)}}{\frac{1}{k} \log _e \frac{(40-30)}{(80-30)}}
$$
On solving we get, time taken to cool down from $62^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to $32^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, $t=8.6$ minutes.

subject: Physics
solution:
Heat available from water cooling 25°C to 0°C: $Q_{available} = 100 \times 4200 \times 25 = 10.5 \times 10^6$ J.
Heat to warm ice from -10°C to 0°C: $Q_1 = 10 \times 2100 \times 10 = 0.21 \times 10^6$ J.
Heat to melt ice at 0°C: $Q_2 = 10 \times 3.36 \times 10^5 = 3.36 \times 10^6$ J.
Total needed: $0.21 + 3.36 = 3.57 \times 10^6$ J.
Since available heat exceeds required heat, all ice melts. Remaining heat: $10.5 - 3.57 = 6.93 \times 10^6$ J
warms 110 kg water: $\Delta T = \frac{6.93 \times 10^6}{110 \times 4200} = 15$ °C.
Final temperature: 15°C.
Decrement in water temperature: $25 - 15 = 10$ °C.

⇒450-TT-0=K2 A2l1 K1 A1l2=9×12×2

⇒450-T=9 T⇒T=45°C

subject: Physics
solution:
Total length at 30°C = 120 cm, each rod = 60 cm
$\alpha_{Al} = 24 \times 10^{-6}$/°C, $\alpha_{Steel} = 1.2 \times 10^{-5}$/°C
$\Delta T = 100 - 30 = 70$°C
$\Delta L_{Al} = 60 \times 24 \times 10^{-6} \times 70 = 0.1008$ cm
$\Delta L_{Steel} = 60 \times 1.2 \times 10^{-5} \times 70 = 0.0504$ cm
Total expansion $= 0.1008 + 0.0504 = 0.1512$ cm
Final length $= 120 + 0.1512 = 120.15$ cm
